definition: The total volume of fresh water used to produce goods and services, encompassing green, blue, and grey water.
A digital record of a product's lifecycle, providing information on its origin, materials, environmental impact, and more.
The volume of rainwater stored in the soil that is evaporated from the soil or transpired from plants.
The volume of surface or groundwater that is evaporated, incorporated into a product, or returned to a different body of water or at a different time.
The volume of fresh water required to assimilate pollutants to meet specific water quality standards.
An international standard providing principles, requirements, and guidelines for water footprint assessment based on life cycle assessment.
A global framework promoting responsible water use and management.
